name: video_kiosk
short_description: Raspberry Pi OS SD card builder that provisions a VLC-based video kiosk with automated setup.
long_description: Video Kiosk is a Bash-based tool that automates the provisioning of Raspberry Pi SD cards with a dedicated video playback system. It downloads the official Raspberry Pi OS Lite image, flashes it to a target block device, configures optional Wi-Fi and SSH access via a .env configuration file, and installs a first-boot script that upgrades the system, installs VLC, provisions SSH users with key-based authentication, and sets up automatic fullscreen video looping. The system supports both interactive and non-interactive modes, includes SSH hardening, and provides a complete hands-off deployment solution for creating standalone video display kiosks.

modules.description: Main provisioning script that downloads Raspberry Pi OS, flashes to device, configures Wi-Fi/SSH, and installs kiosk boot service.
Error handling function that logs error messages and exits with status code 1.
Validates that required system commands (wget, xz, dd, etc.) are available on the host system.
Detects boot and root partition identifiers from the target block device, handling both /dev/sdX and /dev/mmcblk0 naming conventions.
Safely unmounts a mount point if it is currently mounted, used during cleanup.
EXIT trap handler that unmounts the boot and root partitions on script termination.

First-boot script embedded in the SD card that performs system upgrade, installs VLC, configures SSH user with key authentication, hardens sshd, and initiates automatic video looping on subsequent boots.
Creates or updates a sudo user with SSH directory and authorized_keys for key-based authentication on the Raspberry Pi.
Applies SSH security hardening by disabling password authentication, root login, and challenge-response, restricting access to provisioned sudo user.
Template configuration file documenting all available .env variables for image URL, mount points, Wi-Fi settings, SSH configuration, and interactive prompts.
Systemd service unit that runs the kiosk-boot.sh script at every system boot with TTY and journal logging.
